concept.title: 11 Hansiplast procedure

section.title: Description
section.p: The Hansiplast procedure is an automatic recovery procedure in the Windows controller.
If the Windows controller is unable to start up multiple times in a row, the Hansiplast procedure is started automatically.

section.title: Process
section.p: The hansiplast procedure is started after 3 attempts of the Windows Controller to reach the standby state. Example: the software has become corrupt.

An image of the current software is always present on the Windows controller. With the Hansiplast procedure this image is restored. The operator panel displays that the Windows controller is installing. The total procedure takes approximately 1 hour to complete.
In most cases the reinstall will not overwrite the current configuration and settings information.
